behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is loader orchestration between manifest validation and module import (not just schema-check logic). Test should use a real temp plugin directory with invalid `manifest.json` plus an entry module with a visible side effect; assert rejection occurs and side effect never happens. Higher levels rejected: behavior text only requires local plugin loading (`configured directory`), with no subprocess/network/AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugins folder containing one plugin with a missing required manifest field and an entry file that writes a marker file when executed. Run the real loader against that folder. Assert: loader reports manifest validation failure, plugin `onLoad` is not called, and marker file is absent (proving no plugin code executed).

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module handoff: dependency resolver + load executor + hook invoker ordering. A pure unit test of topo-sort alone can miss hook-call sequencing bugs. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is local dependency ordering only; no OS/subprocess/network/AI requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Set up two real plugin folders (A depends on B), each `onLoad` appends its name to a shared in-memory/event log. Run loader and assert call order is `[B, A]`. Also assert both were loaded exactly once.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Behavior combines cycle detection with execution gating (no plugin load/hook execution). Unit graph-test alone can miss integration bug where cycle is detected but loader still runs hooks. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is local dependency graph handling; no real subprocess/network/AI.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugin A depends on B and plugin B depends on A in temp plugin dir; each entry module/hook emits a marker on execution. Run loader and assert cycle error includes both plugins and no markers appear (neither imported nor `onLoad` invoked).

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering across module loader and lifecycle dispatcher. Need real module import execution semantics plus hook invocation in one run. System rejected: behavior text does not require subprocess/OS-specific primitives beyond normal local module import.
behaviors.test_description: Build one plugin whose entry module sets a module-scoped flag or writes an init marker during import, and whose `onLoad` asserts that marker exists. Run loader and assert `onLoad` succeeds only when import side effect already occurred; fail test if hook runs first.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: Requires dependency-aware failure isolation across multiple plugins: hook exception handling + dependency filtering + continued initialization. Unit-testing try/catch alone misses graph-based continuation behavior. Higher levels rejected: behavior text is local plugin orchestration only, with no subprocess/network/AI.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: X (throws in `onLoad`), Y (independent), Z (depends on X), and W (independent). Run loader and assert: X failure is reported; Y and W still initialize; Z is skipped/not initialized due to dependency on failed X.
